pattern: Sorting the array turns 'maximize how many positions beat their original value' into a two-pointer sweep: advance a second pointer only when the current value can strictly beat the smallest not-yet-beaten value.
hints: Only the multiset of values matters, not their original positions -- start by sorting.
Walk the sorted array with a second pointer marking the smallest value you still need to beat; when the current value beats it, that pairing counts and you move the pointer forward.
Every value that fails to beat the pointer's target is wasted on a value already claimed or on itself; the pointer's final position is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: strictly increasing, every element can beat one smaller
all identical values, nothing strictly beats anything
smallest and largest possible values mixed
one large value can only ever beat one thing, not the whole rest of the array
descending input, order must not matter
two distinct values repeated, only the smaller-value copies can score

stress.description: large random array near the length ceiling
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
